input.user_input: My client is planning to acquire a competitor. How do I assess whether the deal will trigger HSR antitrust filing thresholds?

actual:
To assess whether your client’s planned acquisition of a competitor triggers the Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR) premerger notification requirements, you must evaluate the transaction against three primary tests: the Commerce Test, the Size of Transaction Test, and the Size of Person Test. You must also consider whether any exemptions apply. 

The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) adjusts the HSR thresholds annually based on changes in the gross national product. The figures below reflect the 2024 thresholds, which became effective on March 6, 2024.

Here is the step-by-step framework for assessing HSR applicability:

### 1. The Commerce Test
This test is almost always satisfied. It requires that either the acquiring person or the acquired person is engaged in U.S. commerce or in any activity affecting U.S. commerce. 

### 2. The Size of Transaction Test
This is the most critical threshold
